{{神社| 名称=乎加神社| 所在地=滋賀県東近江市神郷9| 祭神=豊遠迦比売命 (配祀)白山比売命| 社格=式内社（小）| 画像=[[画像:Oka_jinja.jpg|250px]]
}} '''乎加神社'''（おかじんじゃ）は[[滋賀県]][[東近江市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 [[トヨウケビメ|豊遠迦比売命]]（豊岡姫命）を主祭神とし、[[菊理媛神|白山比売]]（白山比咩神）を配祀する。 ==概要== 豊遠迦比売命、白山比売命を祀る。神代に五穀蚕麻の原種を民衆に授けた神として崇敬されていた。[[延喜式]]では神崎郡二座のうちの一社。第十九代[[允恭天皇]]の勅によりこの地に奉斉されたと伝えられ、古来から[[皇室]]をはじめ[[領主]]の崇敬が厚かった。旧郡名の[[神崎郡]]の名前の由来としては乎加神社があったためであり、郡名を神前と称え、郷の名を神主、村の名前を神郷と呼ぶのも乎加神社が当地に鎮座するからである、と社殿の由来書に書いている。 本殿の向こうに[[神郷亀塚古墳]]があり、古くはその祭祀場であったと思われる。 ==本殿祭神== *[[トヨウケビメ|豊遠迦比売命]] - 配祀・[[菊理媛神|白山比売]] ==境内== *本殿 - 神明造 間口三間 奥行二間 明治22年の伊勢神宮遷宮時に譲り受けたもので、明治25年に移築されたと伝えられている。有形文化財。 *拝殿 - 入母屋造 間口四間 奥行三間 *境内社（摂社・末社）- 日吉神社 若宮神社 五社神社 八阪神社 若宮神社 ==祭事== *[[4月9日]] ==交通アクセス== *JR[[琵琶湖線]] [[能登川駅]]　徒歩20分 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm 総合文化情報センターだより-乎加神社] *[http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/oumi/bun/oum220601-01.html 阜嵐健-延喜式神社の調査-乎加神社] {{DEFAULTSORT:おかしんしや}} [[Category:式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:東近江市]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: 滋賀県神社庁の外部リンクを追加
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=乎加神社| 所在地=滋賀県東近江市神郷9| 祭神=豊遠迦比売命 (配祀)白山比売命| 社格=式内社（小）| 画像=[[画像:Oka_jinja.jpg|250px]]
}} '''乎加神社'''（おかじんじゃ）は[[滋賀県]][[東近江市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 [[トヨウケビメ|豊遠迦比売命]]（豊岡姫命）を主祭神とし、[[菊理媛神|白山比売]]（白山比咩神）を配祀する。 ==概要== 豊遠迦比売命、白山比売命を祀る。神代に五穀蚕麻の原種を民衆に授けた神として崇敬されていた。[[延喜式]]では神崎郡二座のうちの一社。第十九代[[允恭天皇]]の勅によりこの地に奉斉されたと伝えられ、古来から[[皇室]]をはじめ[[領主]]の崇敬が厚かった。旧郡名の[[神崎郡]]の名前の由来としては乎加神社があったためであり、郡名を神前と称え、郷の名を神主、村の名前を神郷と呼ぶのも乎加神社が当地に鎮座するからである、と社殿の由来書に書いている。 本殿の向こうに[[神郷亀塚古墳]]があり、古くはその祭祀場であったと思われる。 ==本殿祭神== *[[トヨウケビメ|豊遠迦比売命]] - 配祀・[[菊理媛神|白山比売]] ==境内== *本殿 - 神明造 間口三間 奥行二間 明治22年の伊勢神宮遷宮時に譲り受けたもので、明治25年に移築されたと伝えられている。有形文化財。 *拝殿 - 入母屋造 間口四間 奥行三間 *境内社（摂社・末社）- 日吉神社 若宮神社 五社神社 八阪神社 若宮神社 ==祭事== *[[4月9日]] ==交通アクセス== *JR[[琵琶湖線]] [[能登川駅]]　徒歩20分 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm 総合文化情報センターだより-乎加神社] *[http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/oumi/bun/oum220601-01.html 阜嵐健-延喜式神社の調査-乎加神社] *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 滋賀県神社庁-滋賀県の神社-乎加神社] {{DEFAULTSORT:おかしんしや}} [[Category:式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:東近江市]] {{shinto-stub}}

乎加神社 }} '''乎加神社'''（おかじんじゃ）は[[滋賀県]][[東近江市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 [[トヨウケビメ|豊遠迦比売命]]（豊岡姫命）を主祭神とし、[[菊理媛神|白山比売]]（白山比咩神）を配祀する。 ==概要== 豊遠迦比売命、白山比売命を祀る。神代に五穀蚕麻の原種を民衆に授けた神として崇敬されていた。[[延喜式]]では神崎郡二座のうちの一社。第十九代[[允恭天皇]]の勅によりこの地に奉斉されたと伝えられ、古来から[[皇室]]をはじめ[[領主]]の崇敬が厚かった。旧郡名の[[神崎郡]]の名前の由来としては乎加神社があったためであり、郡名を神前と称え、郷の名を神主、村の名前を神郷と呼ぶのも乎加神社が当地に鎮座するからである、と社殿の由来書に書いている。 本殿の向こうに[[神郷亀塚古墳]]があり、古くはその祭祀場であったと思われる。 [[画像:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|250px|乎加神社 社歴]] ==本殿祭神== *[[トヨウケビメ|豊遠迦比売命]] - 配祀・[[菊理媛神|白山比売]] ==境内== [[画像:Oka_jinja.jpg|250px|乎加神社 拝殿]] *本殿 - 神明造 間口三間 奥行二間 明治22年の伊勢神宮遷宮時に譲り受けたもので、明治25年に移築されたと伝えられている。有形文化財。 *拝殿 - 入母屋造 間口四間 奥行三間 *境内社（摂社・末社）- 日吉神社 若宮神社 五社神社 八阪神社 若宮神社 ==祭事== *[[4月9日]] ==交通アクセス== *JR[[琵琶湖線]] [[能登川駅]]　徒歩20分 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm 総合文化情報センターだより-乎加神社] *[http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/oumi/bun/oum220601-01.html 阜嵐健-延喜式神社の調査-乎加神社] *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 滋賀県神社庁-滋賀県の神社-乎加神社] {{DEFAULTSORT:おかしんしや}} [[Category:式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:東近江市]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=乎加神社| 所在地=滋賀県東近江市神郷9| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|10|62|N|136|11|07|E}}| 祭神=豊遠迦比売命 (配祀)白山比売命| 社格=式内社（小）| 本殿=神明造 間口三間 奥行二間| 画像=[[画像:Oka_jinja_torii.jpg|250px|乎加神社 鳥居]]
乎加神社 }} [[乎加神社]]（おかじんじゃ）は[[滋賀県]][[東近江市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。[[トヨウケビメ|豊遠迦比売命]]（豊岡姫命）を主祭神とし、[[菊理媛神|白山比売]]（白山比咩神）を配祀する。 ==概要== 社歴によると、[[トヨウケビメ|豊遠迦比売命]]、[[菊理媛神|白山比売]]を祀る。[[神代]]に五穀蚕麻の原種を民衆に授けた神として崇敬されていた。[[延喜式神名帳]]では、神崎郡二座のうちの一社。もう一社は[[河桁御河邊神社]]。第十九代[[允恭天皇]]の勅によりこの地に奉斉されたと伝えられ、[[衣通姫]]の祈願により鎮座という資料もある。古来から[[皇室]]をはじめ[[領主]]の崇敬が厚かった。旧郡名の[[神崎郡]]の名前の由来としては乎加神社があったためであり、郡名を神前と称え、郷の名を神主、村の名前を神郷と呼ぶのも乎加神社が当地に鎮座するためである、とされている。 [[画像:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|250px|乎加神社 社歴]] 本殿の北側に[[3世紀]]頃の[[前方後方墳]]である[[神郷亀塚古墳]]があり、古くはその[[祭祀]]場であったと思われる。 ==本殿祭神== *[[トヨウケビメ|豊遠迦比売命]] - 配祀・[[菊理媛神|白山比売]] ==境内== [[画像:Oka_jinja.jpg|250px|乎加神社 拝殿]] *本殿 - 神明造 間口三間 奥行二間 明治22年の伊勢神宮遷宮時に譲り受けたもので、明治25年に移築されたと伝えられている。有形文化財。 *拝殿 - 入母屋造 間口四間 奥行三間 *境内社（摂社・末社）- 日吉神社 若宮神社 五社神社 八阪神社 若宮神社 ==祭事== *[[4月9日]] ==交通アクセス== *JR[[琵琶湖線]] [[能登川駅]]　徒歩20分 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm 総合文化情報センターだより-乎加神社] *[http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/oumi/bun/oum220601-01.html 阜嵐健-延喜式神社の調査-乎加神社] *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 滋賀県神社庁-滋賀県の神社-乎加神社] *[http://kamnavi.jp/en/oumi.htm 神奈備・延喜式神名帳・近江國] ==関連項目== *[[神郷亀塚古墳]] *[[神社]] *[[延喜式神名帳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:おかしんしや}} [[Category:式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:東近江市]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=乎加神社| 所在地=滋賀県東近江市神郷9| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|10|62|N|136|11|07|E}}| 祭神=豊遠迦比売命 (配祀)白山比売命| 社格=式内社（小）| 本殿=神明造 間口三間 奥行二間| 画像=[[画像:Oka_jinja_torii.jpg|250px|乎加神社 鳥居]]
乎加神社 }} [[乎加神社]]（おかじんじゃ）は[[滋賀県]][[東近江市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。[[トヨウケビメ|豊遠迦比売命]]（豊岡姫命）を主祭神とし、[[菊理媛神|白山比売]]（白山比咩神）を配祀する。 ==概要== 社歴によると、[[トヨウケビメ|豊遠迦比売命]]、[[菊理媛神|白山比売]]を祀る。[[神代]]に五穀蚕麻の原種を民衆に授けた神として崇敬されていた。[[延喜式神名帳]]では、神崎郡二座のうちの一社。もう一社は[[河桁御河邊神社]]。第十九代[[允恭天皇]]の勅によりこの地に奉斉されたと伝えられ、[[衣通姫]]の祈願により鎮座という資料もある。古来から[[皇室]]をはじめ[[領主]]の崇敬が厚かった。旧郡名の[[神崎郡]]の名前の由来としては乎加神社があったためであり、郡名を神前と称え、郷の名を神主、村の名前を神郷と呼ぶのも乎加神社が当地に鎮座するためである、とされている。 [[画像:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|250px|乎加神社 社歴]] 本殿の北側に[[3世紀]]頃の[[前方後方墳]]である[[神郷亀塚古墳]]があり、古くはその[[祭祀]]場であったと思われる。 ==本殿祭神== *[[トヨウケビメ|豊遠迦比売命]] - 配祀・[[菊理媛神|白山比売]] ==境内== [[画像:Oka_jinja.jpg|250px|乎加神社 拝殿]] *本殿 - 神明造 間口三間 奥行二間 明治22年の伊勢神宮遷宮時に譲り受けたもので、明治25年に移築されたと伝えられている。有形文化財。 *拝殿 - 入母屋造 間口四間 奥行三間 *境内社（摂社・末社）- 日吉神社 若宮神社 五社神社 八阪神社 若宮神社 ==祭事== *[[4月9日]] ==交通アクセス== *JR[[琵琶湖線]] [[能登川駅]]　徒歩20分 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm 総合文化情報センターだより-乎加神社] *[http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/oumi/bun/oum220601-01.html 阜嵐健-延喜式神社の調査-乎加神社] *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 滋賀県神社庁-滋賀県の神社-乎加神社] *[http://kamnavi.jp/en/oumi.htm 神奈備・延喜式神名帳・近江國] ==関連項目== *[[神郷亀塚古墳]] *[[神社]] *[[延喜式神名帳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:おかしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:府県社]] [[Category:東近江市]] {{shinto-stub}}

乎加神社 }} [[乎加神社]]（おかじんじゃ）は[[滋賀県]][[東近江市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。[[トヨウケビメ|豊遠迦比売命]]（豊岡姫命）を主祭神とし、[[菊理媛神|白山比売]]（白山比咩神）を配祀する。 ==概要== 社歴によると、[[トヨウケビメ|豊遠迦比売命]]、[[菊理媛神|白山比売]]を祀る。[[神代]]に五穀蚕麻の原種を民衆に授けた神として崇敬されていた。[[延喜式神名帳]]では、神崎郡二座のうちの一社。もう一社は[[河桁御河邊神社]]。第十九代[[允恭天皇]]の勅によりこの地に奉斉されたと伝えられ、[[衣通姫]]の祈願により鎮座という資料もある。古来から[[皇室]]をはじめ[[領主]]の崇敬が厚かった。旧郡名の[[神崎郡]]の名前の由来としては乎加神社があったためであり、郡名を神前と称え、郷の名を神主、村の名前を神郷と呼ぶのも乎加神社が当地に鎮座するためである、とされている。 [[画像:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|250px|乎加神社 社歴]] 本殿の北側に[[3世紀]]頃の[[前方後方墳]]である[[神郷亀塚古墳]]があり、古くはその[[祭祀]]場であったと思われる。 ==本殿祭神== *[[トヨウケビメ|豊遠迦比売命]] - 配祀・[[菊理媛神|白山比売]] ==境内== [[画像:Oka_jinja.jpg|250px|乎加神社 拝殿]] *本殿 - 神明造 間口三間 奥行二間 明治22年の伊勢神宮遷宮時に譲り受けたもので、明治25年に移築されたと伝えられている。有形文化財。 *拝殿 - 入母屋造 間口四間 奥行三間 *境内社（摂社・末社）- 日吉神社 若宮神社 五社神社 八阪神社 若宮神社 ==祭事== *[[4月9日]] ==交通アクセス== *JR[[琵琶湖線]] [[能登川駅]]　徒歩20分 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm 総合文化情報センターだより-乎加神社] *[http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/oumi/bun/oum220601-01.html 阜嵐健-延喜式神社の調査-乎加神社] *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 滋賀県神社庁-滋賀県の神社-乎加神社] *[http://kamnavi.jp/en/oumi.htm 神奈備・延喜式神名帳・近江國] ==関連項目== *[[神郷亀塚古墳]] *[[神社]] *[[延喜式神名帳]] {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|乎加神社}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おかしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:府県社]] [[Category:東近江市]]

{{神社| 名称=乎加神社| 所在地=滋賀県東近江市神郷町字森9| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|10|62|N|136|11|07|E}}| 祭神=豊遠迦比売命 (配祀)白山比売命| 社格=式内社（小）| 本殿=神明造 間口三間 奥行二間| 画像=[[ファイル:Oka_jinja_torii.jpg|250px|乎加神社 鳥居]]
乎加神社 }} [[乎加神社]]（おかじんじゃ）は[[滋賀県]][[東近江市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。[[トヨウケビメ|豊遠迦比売命]]（豊岡姫命）を主祭神とし、[[菊理媛神|白山比売]]（白山比咩神）を配祀する。 == 概要 == [[神代]]に[[五穀]][[蚕]][[麻]]の原種を民衆に授けた神として崇敬されていた[[トヨウケビメ|豊遠迦比売命]]、[[菊理媛神|白山比売]]を祀っており、[[延喜式神名帳]]では[[近江国]][[神崎郡 (滋賀県)|神崎郡]]二座のうちの一座である（もう一座は[[河桁御河邊神社]]）。第十九代[[允恭天皇]]の[[勅命]]によりこの地に祀られ{{要出典範囲|[[衣通姫]]の祈願により鎮座という資料もある。|date=2010年6月}}、古来から[[皇室]]をはじめ[[領主]]の崇敬が厚かったと社歴に記されている。また、「神崎郡」や「神郷」、古代の郷村名「神主」といった地名はいずれも乎加神社に由来するものである。 [[ファイル:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|乎加神社 社歴]] 本殿の北側に[[3世紀]]頃の[[前方後方墳]]である[[神郷亀塚古墳]]があり、{{要出典範囲|古くはその[[祭祀]]場であったと思われる。|date=2010年6月}} == 本殿祭神 == * [[トヨウケビメ|豊遠迦比売命]] - 配祀・[[菊理媛神|白山比売]] == 境内 == [[ファイル:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|乎加神社 拝殿]] 2003年、本殿[http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=101&item_id=00003249 国指定文化財等データベース]、文化庁、2011年4月22日閲覧。・拝殿[http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=101&item_id=00003253 国指定文化財等データベース]、文化庁、2011年4月22日閲覧・幣殿[http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=101&item_id=00003253 国指定文化財等データベース]、文化庁、2011年4月22日閲覧。・祝詞殿[http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=101&item_id=00003251 国指定文化財等データベース]、文化庁、2011年4月22日閲覧。・手水舎[http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=101&item_id=00003251 国指定文化財等データベース]、文化庁、2011年4月22日閲覧。・玉垣[http://www.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=101&item_id=00003252 国指定文化財等データベース]、文化庁、2011年4月22日閲覧。が国の登録有形文化財に指定されている。 * 本殿 - 神明造。間口三間、奥行二間。明治22年の伊勢神宮遷宮時に譲り受けたもので、明治25年に移築されたと伝えられている。 * 拝殿 - 入母屋造。間口四間、奥行三間。 * 境内社（摂社・末社）- 日吉神社・若宮神社・五社神社・八阪神社・若宮神社 == 参考文献 == * 有限会社平凡社地方資料センター『近江・若狭・越前 寺院神社大事典』平凡社 199706 ISBN 4582134033 == 祭事 == * [[4月9日]]に近い日曜日。 == 交通アクセス == * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[東海道本線]]（[[琵琶湖線]]）[[能登川駅]]から徒歩20分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm 総合文化情報センターだより-乎加神社] * [http://www.geocities.jp/engisiki/oumi/bun/oum220601-01.html 阜嵐健-延喜式神社の調査-乎加神社] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 滋賀県神社庁-滋賀県の神社-乎加神社] * [http://kamnavi.jp/en/oumi.htm 神奈備・延喜式神名帳・近江國] == 関連項目 == * [[神郷亀塚古墳]] * [[延喜式神名帳]] {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|乎加神社}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おかしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:府県社]] [[Category:東近江市]]

神明造。間口三間、奥行二間。明治22年の伊勢神宮遷宮時に譲り受けたもので、明治25年に移築されたと伝えられている。 * 拝殿 - 入母屋造。間口三間、奥行三間。床や建具、壁を設けない吹き放しの建築である。 * 境内社（摂社・末社）- 日吉神社・若宮神社・五社神社・八阪神社・若宮神社 == 参考文献 == * 有限会社平凡社地方資料センター『近江・若狭・越前 寺院神社大事典』平凡社 199706 ISBN 4582134033 == 祭事 == * [[4月9日]]に近い日曜日。 == 交通アクセス == * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[東海道本線]]（[[琵琶湖線]]）[[能登川駅]]から徒歩20分 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm 総合文化情報センターだより-乎加神社] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 滋賀県神社庁-滋賀県の神社-乎加神社] == 関連項目 == * [[神郷亀塚古墳]] * [[延喜式神名帳]] {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|乎加神社}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おかしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:東近江市]]

{{神社| 名称=乎加神社| 所在地=滋賀県東近江市神郷町字森9| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|10|62|N|136|11|07|E}}| 祭神=豊遠迦比売命 (配祀)白山比売命| 社格=式内社（小）、県社| 本殿=神明造 間口三間 奥行二間| 画像=[[ファイル:Oka_jinja_torii.jpg|250px|乎加神社 鳥居]]
乎加神社 }} '''乎加神社'''（おかじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[東近江市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。[[トヨウケビメ|豊遠迦比売命]]（豊岡姫命）を主祭神とし、[[菊理媛神|白山比売]]（白山比咩神）を配祀する。 == 概要 == [[神代]]に[[五穀]][[蚕]][[麻]]の原種を民衆に授けた神として崇敬されていた[[トヨウケビメ|豊遠迦比売命]]、[[菊理媛神|白山比売]]を祀っており、[[延喜式神名帳]]では[[近江国]][[神崎郡 (滋賀県)|神崎郡]]二座のうちの一座である（もう一座は[[河桁御河邊神社]]）。第十九代[[允恭天皇]]の[[勅命]]によりこの地に祀られ{{要出典範囲|[[衣通姫]]の祈願により鎮座という資料もある。|date=2010年6月}}、古来から[[皇室]]をはじめ[[領主]]の崇敬が厚かったと社歴に記されている。また、「神崎郡」や「神郷」、古代の郷村名「神主」といった地名はいずれも乎加神社に由来するものである。 [[ファイル:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|乎加神社 社歴]] 本殿の北側に[[3世紀]]頃の[[前方後方墳]]である[[神郷亀塚古墳]]があり、{{要出典範囲|古くはその[[祭祀]]場であったと思われる。|date=2010年6月}} == 本殿祭神 == * [[トヨウケビメ|豊遠迦比売命]] - 配祀・[[菊理媛神|白山比売]] == 境内 == [[ファイル:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|乎加神社 幣殿]] 2003年、本殿[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=101&item_id=00003249 国指定文化財等データベース]、文化庁、2011年4月22日閲覧。・拝殿[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=101&item_id=00003253 国指定文化財等データベース]、文化庁、2011年4月22日閲覧・幣殿[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=101&item_id=00003253 国指定文化財等データベース]、文化庁、2011年4月22日閲覧。・祝詞殿[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=101&item_id=00003251 国指定文化財等データベース]、文化庁、2011年4月22日閲覧。・手水舎[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=101&item_id=00003251 国指定文化財等データベース]、文化庁、2011年4月22日閲覧。・玉垣[http://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/maindetails.asp?register_id=101&item_id=00003252 国指定文化財等データベース]、文化庁、2011年4月22日閲覧。が国の[[登録有形文化財]]に登録されている。いずれも明治時代の建築。 * 本殿 - 神明造。間口三間、奥行二間。明治22年の伊勢神宮遷宮時に譲り受けたもので、明治25年に移築されたと伝えられている。 * 拝殿 - 入母屋造。間口三間、奥行三間。床や建具、壁を設けない吹き放しの建築である。 * 境内社（摂社・末社）- 日吉神社・若宮神社・五社神社・八阪神社・若宮神社 == 祭事 == * [[4月9日]]に近い日曜日。 == 交通アクセス == * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[東海道本線]]（[[琵琶湖線]]）[[能登川駅]]から徒歩20分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『近江・若狭・越前 寺院神社大事典』、有限会社平凡社地方資料センター、[[平凡社]]、1997年6月 ISBN 4582134033 == 関連項目 == * [[神郷亀塚古墳]] * [[延喜式神名帳]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm 総合文化情報センターだより-乎加神社] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 滋賀県神社庁-滋賀県の神社-乎加神社] {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|乎加神社}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おかしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:東近江市の建築物]] [[Category:東近江市の歴史]]

{{神社 | 名称=乎加神社 | 所在地=滋賀県東近江市神郷町字森9 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|10|62|N|136|11|07|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=豊遠迦比売命
(配祀)白山比売命 | 社格=式内社（小）、県社 | 本殿=神明造 間口三間 奥行二間 | 画像=[[ファイル:Oka_jinja_torii.jpg|250px|乎加神社 鳥居]]
乎加神社 }} '''乎加神社'''（おかじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[東近江市]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。[[トヨウケビメ|豊遠迦比売命]]（豊岡姫命）を主祭神とし、[[菊理媛神|白山比売]]（白山比咩神）を配祀する。 == 概要 == [[神代]]に[[五穀]][[蚕]][[麻]]の原種を民衆に授けた神として崇敬されていた[[トヨウケビメ|豊遠迦比売命]]、[[菊理媛神|白山比売]]を祀っており、[[延喜式神名帳]]では[[近江国]][[神崎郡 (滋賀県)|神崎郡]]二座のうちの一座である（もう一座は[[河桁御河邊神社]]）。第十九代[[允恭天皇]]の[[勅命]]によりこの地に祀られ{{要出典範囲|[[衣通姫]]の祈願により鎮座という資料もある。|date=2010年6月}}、古来から[[皇室]]をはじめ[[領主]]の崇敬が厚かったと社歴に記されている。また、「神崎郡」や「神郷」、古代の郷村名「神主」といった地名はいずれも乎加神社に由来するものである。 [[ファイル:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|乎加神社 社歴]] 本殿の北側に[[3世紀]]頃の[[前方後方墳]]である[[神郷亀塚古墳]]があり、{{要出典範囲|古くはその[[祭祀]]場であったと思われる。|date=2010年6月}} == 本殿祭神 == * [[トヨウケビメ|豊遠迦比売命]] - 配祀・[[菊理媛神|白山比売]] == 境内 == [[ファイル:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|乎加神社 幣殿]] 2003年、本殿[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 国指定文化財等データベース]、文化庁、2011年4月22日閲覧。・拝殿[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 国指定文化財等データベース]、文化庁、2011年4月22日閲覧・幣殿[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 国指定文化財等データベース]、文化庁、2011年4月22日閲覧。・祝詞殿[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 国指定文化財等データベース]、文化庁、2011年4月22日閲覧。・手水舎[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 国指定文化財等データベース]、文化庁、2011年4月22日閲覧。・玉垣[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 国指定文化財等データベース]、文化庁、2011年4月22日閲覧。が国の[[登録有形文化財]]に登録されている。いずれも明治時代の建築。 * 本殿 - 神明造。間口三間、奥行二間。明治22年の伊勢神宮遷宮時に譲り受けたもので、明治25年に移築されたと伝えられている。 * 拝殿 - 入母屋造。間口三間、奥行三間。床や建具、壁を設けない吹き放しの建築である。 * 境内社（摂社・末社）- 日吉神社・若宮神社・五社神社・八阪神社・若宮神社 == 祭事 == * [[4月9日]]に近い日曜日。 == 交通アクセス == * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[東海道本線]]（[[琵琶湖線]]）[[能登川駅]]から徒歩20分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 『近江・若狭・越前 寺院神社大事典』、有限会社平凡社地方資料センター、[[平凡社]]、1997年6月 ISBN 4582134033 == 関連項目 == * [[神郷亀塚古墳]] * [[延喜式神名帳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|乎加神社}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm 総合文化情報センターだより-乎加神社] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 滋賀県神社庁-滋賀県の神社-乎加神社] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おかしんしや}} [[Category:滋賀県の神社]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:東近江市の建築物]] [[Category:東近江市の歴史]]

{{Shrine | Name=Oka Shrine | Location=Moricho, Jingocho, Higashiomi City, Shiga Prefecture | Coordinates={{coord|35|10|37.4|N|136|11|3.7|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deity=Toyouke no Mikoto
(Associate Deity) Hakusan Hime no Mikoto | Rank=Shikinaisha (Minor), Ken-sha | Main Hall=Shinmei-zukuri, Width: 3 ken, Depth: 2 ken | Image=[[File:Oka_jinja_torii.jpg|250px|Oka Shrine Torii]]
Oka Shrine }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiomi|ja|東近江市}}, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} as the main deity, along with {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}}, a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}}. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province|ja|神崎郡}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Late Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} burial mound, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} * Associate Deity: {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * **Main Hall**: Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * **Haiden**: Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * **Shrine Precincts**: Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shiga Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiomi]] [[Category:History of Higashiomi]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Shiga Prefecture]] [[ja:乎加神社]]

Oka Shrine }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiomi|ja|東近江市}}, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} as the main deity, along with {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}}, a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}}. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province|ja|神崎郡}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Late Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} burial mound, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} * Associate Deity: {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * **Main Hall**: Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * **Haiden**: Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * **Shrine Precincts**: Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shiga Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiomi]] [[Category:History of Higashiomi]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Shiga Prefecture]] [[ja:乎加神社]]

Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province|ja|神崎郡}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Late Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} burial mound, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} * Associate Deity: {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiomi]] [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:History of Higashiomi]] [[Category:Last 100]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:乎加神社]]

{{Shrine | Name=Oka Shrine | Location=Moricho, Jingocho, Higashiomi City, Shiga Prefecture | Coordinates={{coord|35|10|37.4|N|136|11|3.7|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deity=Toyouke no Mikoto
(Associate Deity) Hakusan Hime no Mikoto | Rank=Shikinaisha (Minor), Ken-sha | Main Hall=Shinmei-zukuri, Width: 3 ken, Depth: 2 ken | Image=[[File:Oka_jinja_torii.jpg|250px|Oka Shrine Torii]]
Oka Shrine }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiomi|ja|東近江市}}, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} as the main deity, along with {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}}, a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}}. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province|ja|神崎郡}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Late Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} burial mound, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} * Associate Deity: {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiomi]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Higashiomi]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[ja:乎加神社]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} as the main deity, along with {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}}, a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}}. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province|ja|神崎郡}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Late Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} burial mound, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} * Associate Deity: {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiomi]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Higashiomi]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[ja:乎加神社]]

Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiomi]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Higashiomi]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[ja:乎加神社]]

{{Shrine | name=Oka Shrine | location=Moricho, Jingocho, Higashiomi City, Shiga Prefecture | coordinates={{coord|35|10|37.4|N|136|11|3.7|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deity=Toyouke no Mikoto
(Associate Deity) Hakusan Hime no Mikoto | Rank=Shikinaisha (Minor), Ken-sha | Main Hall=Shinmei-zukuri, Width: 3 ken, Depth: 2 ken | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Oka_jinja_torii.jpg|250px|Oka Shrine Torii]]
Oka Shrine }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiomi|ja|東近江市}}, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} as the main deity, along with {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}}, a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}}. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province|ja|神崎郡}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Late Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} burial mound, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} * Associate Deity: {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiomi|ja|東近江市}}, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} as the main deity, along with {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}}, a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}}. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province|ja|神崎郡}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Late Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} burial mound, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} * Associate Deity: {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Shiga Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiomi]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:History of Higashiomi]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[ja:乎加神社]]

Oka Shrine }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiomi|ja|東近江市}}, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} as the main deity, along with {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}}, a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}}. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province|ja|神崎郡}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Late Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} burial mound, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} * Associate deity: {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Oka Shrine | location=Moricho, Jingocho, Higashiomi City, Shiga Prefecture | coordinates={{coord|35|10|37.4|N|136|11|3.7|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Toyouke no Mikoto
(Associate deity) Hakusan Hime no Mikoto | Rank=Shikinaisha (Minor), Ken-sha | Main Hall=Shinmei-zukuri, Width: 3 ken, Depth: 2 ken | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Oka_jinja_torii.jpg|250px|Oka Shrine Torii]]
Oka Shrine }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiomi|ja|東近江市}}, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} as the main deity, along with {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}}, a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}}. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province|ja|神崎郡}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Late Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} burial mound, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} * Associate deity: {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Oka Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Shiga Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Oka Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Oka Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Oka Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiomi]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Oka Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:History of Higashiomi]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Oka Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[sn:Oka Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Oka Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:乎加神社]]

(Associate deity) Hakusan Hime no Mikoto | Rank=Shikinaisha (Minor), Ken-sha | Main Hall=Shinmei-zukuri, Width: 3 ken, Depth: 2 ken | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Oka_jinja_torii.jpg|250px|Oka Shrine Torii]]
Oka Shrine }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiomi|ja|東近江市}}, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} as the main deity, along with {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}}, a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}}. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province|ja|神崎郡}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Late Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} burial mound, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} * Associate deity: {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Shiga Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiomi]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:History of Higashiomi]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[sn:Oka Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Oka Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:乎加神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Oka Shrine | location=Moricho, Jingocho, Higashiomi City, Shiga Prefecture | coordinates={{coord|35|10|37.4|N|136|11|3.7|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Toyouke no Mikoto
(Associate deity) Hakusan Hime no Mikoto | Rank=Shikinaisha (Minor), Ken-sha | Main Hall=Shinmei-zukuri, Width: 3 ken, Depth: 2 ken | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Oka_jinja_torii.jpg|250px|Oka Shrine Torii]]
Oka Shrine }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiomi|ja|東近江市}}, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} as the main deity, along with {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}}, a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}}. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province|ja|神崎郡}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Late Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} burial mound, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} * Associate deity: {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oka Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oka Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Shiga Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oka Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oka Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oka Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oka Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oka Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oka Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiomi]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oka Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oka Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:History of Higashiomi]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oka Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Oka Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[Category:Oka Shrine]][[sn:Oka Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Oka Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:乎加神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Oka Shrine | location=Moricho, Jingocho, Higashiomi City, Shiga Prefecture | coordinates={{coord|35|10|37.4|N|136|11|3.7|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Toyouke no Mikoto
(Associate deity) Hakusan Hime no Mikoto | Rank=Shikinaisha (Minor), Ken-sha | Main Hall=Shinmei-zukuri, Width: 3 ken, Depth: 2 ken | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Oka_jinja_torii.jpg|250px|Oka Shrine Torii]]
Oka Shrine }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiomi|ja|東近江市}}, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} as the main deity, along with {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}}, a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}}. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province|ja|神崎郡}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Late Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} burial mound, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} * Associate deity: {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Oka {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Oka Shrine | location=Moricho, Jingocho, Higashiomi City, Shiga Prefecture | coordinates={{coord|35|10|37.4|N|136|11|3.7|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Toyouke no Mikoto
(Associate deity) Hakusan Hime no Mikoto | Rank=Shikinaisha (Minor), Ken-sha | Main Hall=Shinmei-zukuri, Width: 3 ken, Depth: 2 ken | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Oka_jinja_torii.jpg|250px|Oka Shrine Torii]]
Oka Shrine }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiomi|ja|東近江市}}, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} as the main deity, along with {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}}, a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}}. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province|ja|神崎郡}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Late Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} burial mound, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} * Associate deity: {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Oka {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Oka Shrine | location=Moricho, Jingocho, Higashiomi City, Shiga Prefecture | coordinates={{coord|35|10|37.4|N|136|11|3.7|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Toyouke no Mikoto
(Associate deity) Hakusan Hime no Mikoto | Rank=Shikinaisha (Minor), Ken-sha | Main Hall=Shinmei-zukuri, Width: 3 ken, Depth: 2 ken | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Oka_jinja_torii.jpg|250px|Oka Shrine Torii]]
Oka Shrine }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiomi|ja|東近江市}}, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} as the main deity, along with {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}}, a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}}. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province|ja|神崎郡}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Late Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} * Associate deity: {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Oka Oka Shrine }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiomi|ja|東近江市}}, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} as the main deity, along with {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}}, a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}}. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province|ja|神崎郡}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Late Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} * Associate deity: {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] *
