[[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Late Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} * Associate deity: {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} == Oka Shrine }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiomi|ja|東近江市}}, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} as the main deity, along with {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}}, a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}}. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province|ja|神崎郡}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Late Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} * Associate deity: {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}. == References == {{Reflist}}== See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Late Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} * Associate deity: {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also! == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} == Oka Shrine }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiomi|ja|東近江市}}, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} as the main deity, along with {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}}, a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}}. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province|ja|神崎郡}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Late Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} * Associate deity: {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Oka [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Late Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} * Associate deity: {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} == Oka Shrine }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiomi|ja|東近江市}}, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, previously ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} as the main deity, along with {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}}, a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}}. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province|ja|神崎郡}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Late Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} * Associate deity: {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Late Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} * Associate deity: {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Oka Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiomi]] [[Category:History of Higashiomi]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Oka Shrine]] [[ja:乎加神社]] [[en:Oka Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Oka Shrine}}

Oka Shrine }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Higashiomi|ja|東近江市|12=draft}}, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}}, previously ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ|12=draft}} as the main deity, along with {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神|12=draft}} as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ|12=draft}}, a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神|12=draft}}. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province|ja|神崎郡|12=draft}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社|12=draft}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=draft}}, a {{ill|Late Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}} Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ|12=draft}} * Associate deity: {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神|12=draft}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財|12=draft}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Oka Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiomi]] [[Category:History of Higashiomi]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Oka Shrine]] [[ja:乎加神社]] [[en:Oka Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Oka Shrine}}

Oka Shrine }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Higashiomi|ja|東近江市|12=draft}}, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}}, previously ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ|12=draft}} as the main deity, along with {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神|12=draft}} as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ|12=draft}}, a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神|12=draft}}. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province|ja|神崎郡|12=draft}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社|12=draft}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=draft}}, a {{ill|Late Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}} Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ|12=draft}} * Associate deity: {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神|12=draft}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財|12=draft}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Oka Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiomi]] [[Category:History of Higashiomi]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Oka Shrine]] [[ja:乎加神社]] [[en:Oka Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Oka Shrine}}

Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Oka Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiomi]] [[Category:History of Higashiomi]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Oka Shrine]] [[ja:乎加神社]] [[en:Oka Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Oka Shrine}}

(Associate deity) Hakusan Hime no Mikoto | Rank=Shikinaisha (Minor), Ken-sha | Main Hall=Shinmei-zukuri, Width: 3 ken, Depth: 2 ken | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Oka_jinja_torii.jpg|250px|Oka Shrine Torii]]
Oka Shrine }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Higashiomi|ja|東近江市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, previously Ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ|12=draft|12=draft}} as the main deity, along with {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神|12=draft|12=draft}} as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ|12=draft|12=draft}}, a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神|12=draft|12=draft}}. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province|ja|神崎郡|12=draft|12=draft}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社|12=draft|12=draft}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, a {{ill|Late Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft|12=draft}} Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ|12=draft|12=draft}} * Associate deity: {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神|12=draft|12=draft}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財|12=draft|12=draft}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Oka Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiomi]] [[Category:History of Higashiomi]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Oka Shrine]] [[ja:乎加神社]] [[en:Oka Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Oka Shrine}}

Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線|12=draft|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Oka Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiomi]] [[Category:History of Higashiomi]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Oka Shrine]] [[ja:乎加神社]] [[en:Oka Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Oka Shrine}}

(Associate deity) Hakusan Hime no Mikoto | Rank=Shikinaisha (Minor), Ken-sha | Main Hall=Shinmei-zukuri, Width: 3 ken, Depth: 2 ken | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Oka_jinja_torii.jpg|250px|Oka Shrine Torii]]
Oka Shrine }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Higashiomi|ja|東近江市}}, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha}}, previously Ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} as the main deity, along with {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}}, a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}}. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province|ja|神崎郡}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}}, a {{ill|Late Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代}} Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ}} * Associate deity: {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Oka Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiomi]] [[Category:History of Higashiomi]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Oka Shrine]] [[ja:乎加神社]] [[en:Oka Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Oka Shrine}}

[[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=draft}}, a {{ill|Late Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|12=draft}} Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ|12=draft}} * Associate deity: {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神|12=draft}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財|12=draft}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線|12=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|乎加神社|version=91213086|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Oka Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiomi]] [[Category:History of Higashiomi]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Shiga Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Oka Shrine]] [[ja:乎加神社]] [[en:Oka Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Oka Shrine}}

According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. (Associate deity) Hakusan Hime no Mikoto | Rank=Shikinaisha (Minor), Ken-sha | Main Hall=Shinmei-zukuri, Width: 3 ken, Depth: 2 ken | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Oka_jinja_torii.jpg|250px|Oka Shrine Torii]]
Oka Shrine }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Higashiomi|ja|東近江市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, previously Ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as the main deity, along with {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province|ja|神崎郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a {{ill|Late Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Associate deity: {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|乎加神社|version=91213086|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Oka Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiomi]] [[Category:History of Higashiomi]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Shiga Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Oka Shrine]] [[ja:乎加神社]] [[en:Oka Shrine]]

The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke no Mikoto|qq=}} as the main deity, along with {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|qq=}} as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke no Mikoto|qq=}}, a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|qq=}}. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province|ja|神崎郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province|qq=}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|qq=}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|qq=}}, a {{ill|Late Yayoi period|ja|弥生時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Yayoi period|qq=}} Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke no Mikoto|ja|トヨウケビメ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke no Mikoto|qq=}} * Associate deity: {{ill|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|ja|菊理媛神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|qq=}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|qq=}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|ja|能登川駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Notogawa Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|ja|琵琶湖線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Biwako Line|qq=}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|乎加神社|version=91213086|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Oka Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiomi]] [[Category:History of Higashiomi]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Shiga Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Oka Shrine]] [[ja:乎加神社]] [[en:Oka Shrine]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, previously Ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as one of the two shrines in [[:en:Kanzaki District, Hyōgo|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province]] (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|qq=}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|qq=}}, a [[:en:Yayoi period|Late Yayoi period]] Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Mikoto]] * Associate deity: [[:en:Kukurihime|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto]] == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|qq=}}. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as one of the two shrines in [[:en:Kanzaki District, Hyōgo|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province]] (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|qq=}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|qq=}}, a [[:en:Yayoi period|Late Yayoi period]] Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Mikoto]] * Associate deity: [[:en:Kukurihime|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto]] == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|qq=}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Notogawa Station|Notogawa Station]] on the [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|乎加神社|version=91213086|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiomi]] [[Category:History of Higashiomi]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Shiga Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Oka Shrine]] [[ja:乎加神社]] [[en:Oka Shrine]] [[Category:東近江市の建築物]]

Originally part of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Notogawa Station|Notogawa Station]] on the [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|乎加神社|version=91213086|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiomi]] [[Category:History of Higashiomi]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Shiga Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Oka Shrine]] [[ja:乎加神社]] [[en:Oka Shrine]] [[Category:東近江市の建築物]] [[Category:東近江市の歴史]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:滋賀県の登録有形文化財]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Oka Shrine | location=Moricho, Jingocho, Higashiomi City, Shiga Prefecture | coordinates={{coord|35|10|37.4|N|136|11|3.7|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Toyouke no Mikoto
(Associate deity) Hakusan Hime no Mikoto | Rank=Shikinaisha (Minor), Ken-sha | Main Hall=Shinmei-zukuri, Width: 3 ken, Depth: 2 ken | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Oka_jinja_torii.jpg|250px|Oka Shrine Torii]]
Oka Shrine }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Higashiōmi|Higashiomi]], Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, previously Ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Mikoto]] as the main deity, along with [[:en:Kukurihime|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto]] as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Mikoto]], a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as [[:en:Kukurihime|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto]]. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as one of the two shrines in [[:en:Kanzaki District, Hyōgo|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province]] (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|qq=}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|qq=}}, a [[:en:Yayoi period|Late Yayoi period]] Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Mikoto]] * Associate deity: [[:en:Kukurihime|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto]] == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|qq=}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Notogawa Station|Notogawa Station]] on the [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|乎加神社|version=91213086|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiomi]] [[Category:History of Higashiomi]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Shiga Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Oka Shrine]] [[ja:乎加神社]] [[en:Oka Shrine]] [[Category:東近江市の建築物]] [[Category:東近江市の歴史]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:滋賀県の登録有形文化財]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Oka Shrine | location=Moricho, Jingocho, Higashiomi City, Shiga Prefecture | coordinates={{coord|35|10|37.4|N|136|11|3.7|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Toyouke no Mikoto
(Associate deity) Hakusan Hime no Mikoto | Rank=Shikinaisha (Minor), Ken-sha | Main Hall=Shinmei-zukuri, Width: 3 ken, Depth: 2 ken | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Oka_jinja_torii.jpg|250px|Oka Shrine Torii]]
Oka Shrine }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Higashiōmi|Higashiomi]], Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, previously Ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Mikoto]] as the main deity, along with [[:en:Kukurihime|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto]] as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Mikoto]], a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as [[:en:Kukurihime|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto]]. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as one of the two shrines in [[:en:Kanzaki District, Hyōgo|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province]] (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|qq=}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|qq=}}, a [[:en:Yayoi period|Late Yayoi period]] Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Mikoto]] * Associate deity: [[:en:Kukurihime|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto]] == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|qq=}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Notogawa Station|Notogawa Station]] on the [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|乎加神社|version=91213086|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiomi]] [[Category:History of Higashiomi]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Properties of Shiga Prefecture]] }} [[sn:Oka Shrine]] [[ja:乎加神社]] [[en:Oka Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings in Higashiomi, Shiga]] [[Category:History of Higashiomi, Shiga]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan in Shiga prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]]

The shrine enshrines [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Mikoto]] as the main deity, along with [[:en:Kukurihime|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto]] as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Mikoto]], a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as [[:en:Kukurihime|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto]]. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as one of the two shrines in [[:en:Kanzaki District, Hyōgo|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province]] (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|qq=}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|qq=}}, a [[:en:Yayoi period|Late Yayoi period]] Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Mikoto]] * Associate deity: [[:en:Kukurihime|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto]] == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|qq=}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Notogawa Station|Notogawa Station]] on the [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Originally part of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Notogawa Station|Notogawa Station]] on the [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|乎加神社|version=91213086|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[sn:Oka Shrine]] [[ja:乎加神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Higashiomi, Shiga]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan in Shiga prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Oka-jinja (Shiga)}}
