[[sn:commons:Category:Oka-jinja (Shiga)]][[ja:乎加神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11369798]] Wikidata name: Oka-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Oka Shrine | location=Moricho, Jingocho, Higashiomi City, Shiga Prefecture | coordinates={{Coord|35.177055555555555|N|136.18436111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Toyouke no Mikoto
(Associate deity) Hakusan Hime no Mikoto | Rank=Shikinaisha (Minor), Ken-sha | Main Hall=Shinmei-zukuri, Width: 3 ken, Depth: 2 ken | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Oka jinja torii.jpg | prefecture={{ill|Higashiomi|WD=Q240891}} }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Higashiōmi|Higashiomi]], Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, previously Ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Mikoto]] as the main deity, along with [[:en:Kukurihime|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto]] as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Mikoto]], a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as [[:en:Kukurihime|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto]]. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as one of the two shrines in [[:en:Kanzaki District, Hyōgo|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province]] (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|1=Kawaketamikabe Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553975}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|1=Jingō Kamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590941}}, a [[:en:Yayoi period|Late Yayoi period]] Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Mikoto]] * Associate deity: [[:en:Kukurihime|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto]] == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Notogawa Station|Notogawa Station]] on the [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|1=Jingō Kamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590941}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Originally part of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Notogawa Station|Notogawa Station]] on the [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|1=Jingō Kamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590941}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|乎加神社|version=91213086|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[ja:乎加神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Higashiomi, Shiga]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan in Shiga prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Oka-jinja (Shiga)}} {{wikidata link|Q11369798}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Oka-jinja (Shiga)]][[ja:乎加神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11369798]] Wikidata name: Oka-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Oka Shrine | location=Moricho, Jingocho, Higashiomi City, Shiga Prefecture | coordinates={{Coord|35.177055555555555|N|136.18436111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Toyouke no Mikoto
(Associate deity) Hakusan Hime no Mikoto | Rank=Shikinaisha (Minor), Ken-sha | Main Hall=Shinmei-zukuri, Width: 3 ken, Depth: 2 ken | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Oka jinja torii.jpg | prefecture={{ill|Higashiomi|WD=Q240891}} }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Higashiōmi|Higashiomi]], Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, previously Ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Mikoto]] as the main deity, along with [[:en:Kukurihime|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto]] as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Mikoto]], a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as [[:en:Kukurihime|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto]]. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as one of the two shrines in [[:en:Kanzaki District, Hyōgo|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province]] (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|1=Kawaketamikabe Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553975}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|1=Jingō Kamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590941}}, a [[:en:Yayoi period|Late Yayoi period]] Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Mikoto]] * Associate deity: [[:en:Kukurihime|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto]] == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Notogawa Station|Notogawa Station]] on the [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|1=Jingō Kamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590941}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|乎加神社|version=91213086|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[ja:乎加神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Higashiomi, Shiga]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan in Shiga prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Oka-jinja (Shiga)}} {{wikidata link|Q11369798}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Originally part of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Notogawa Station|Notogawa Station]] on the [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|1=Jingō Kamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590941}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|乎加神社|version=91213086|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[ja:乎加神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Higashiomi, Shiga]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan in Shiga prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Oka-jinja (Shiga)}} {{wikidata link|Q11369798}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[commons:Category:Oka-jinja (Shiga)]][[ja:乎加神社]] [[commons:Category:Oka-jinja (Shiga)]][[ja:乎加神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Oka-jinja (Shiga)]][[ja:乎加神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11369798]] Wikidata name: Oka-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Oka Shrine | location=Moricho, Jingocho, Higashiomi City, Shiga Prefecture | coordinates={{Coord|35.177055555555555|N|136.18436111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Toyouke no Mikoto
(Associate deity) Hakusan Hime no Mikoto | Rank=Shikinaisha (Minor), Ken-sha | Main Hall=Shinmei-zukuri, Width: 3 ken, Depth: 2 ken | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Oka jinja torii.jpg | prefecture={{ill|Higashiomi|WD=Q240891}} }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Higashiōmi|Higashiomi]], Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, previously Ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Mikoto]] as the main deity, along with [[:en:Kukurihime|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto]] as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Mikoto]], a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as [[:en:Kukurihime|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto]]. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as one of the two shrines in [[:en:Kanzaki District, Hyōgo|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province]] (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|1=Kawaketamikabe Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553975}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|1=Jingō Kamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590941}}, a [[:en:Yayoi period|Late Yayoi period]] Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Mikoto]] * Associate deity: [[:en:Kukurihime|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto]] == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Notogawa Station|Notogawa Station]] on the [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|1=Jingō Kamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590941}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Originally part of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Notogawa Station|Notogawa Station]] on the [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|1=Jingō Kamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590941}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - [[commons:Category:Oka-jinja (Shiga)]][[ja:乎加神社]] [[commons:Category:Oka-jinja (Shiga)]][[ja:乎加神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Oka-jinja (Shiga)]][[ja:乎加神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11369798]] Wikidata name: Oka-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Oka Shrine | location=Moricho, Jingocho, Higashiomi City, Shiga Prefecture | coordinates={{Coord|35.177055555555555|N|136.18436111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Toyouke no Mikoto
(Associate deity) Hakusan Hime no Mikoto | Rank=Shikinaisha (Minor), Ken-sha | Main Hall=Shinmei-zukuri, Width: 3 ken, Depth: 2 ken | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Oka jinja torii.jpg | prefecture={{ill|Higashiomi|WD=Q240891}} }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Higashiōmi|Higashiomi]], Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, previously Ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Mikoto]] as the main deity, along with [[:en:Kukurihime|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto]] as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Mikoto]], a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as [[:en:Kukurihime|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto]]. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as one of the two shrines in [[:en:Kanzaki District, Hyōgo|Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province]] (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|1=Kawaketamikabe Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553975}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|1=Jingō Kamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590941}}, a [[:en:Yayoi period|Late Yayoi period]] Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Mikoto]] * Associate deity: [[:en:Kukurihime|Hakusan Hime no Mikoto]] == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Notogawa Station|Notogawa Station]] on the [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|1=Jingō Kamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590941}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Originally part of the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from [[:en:Notogawa Station|Notogawa Station]] on the [[:en:Biwako Line|Biwako Line]]. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|1=Jingō Kamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590941}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|乎加神社|version=91213086|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:乎加神社]] {{Commons category|Oka-jinja (Shiga)}} {{wikidata link|Q11369798}} [[Category:Buildings in Higashiomi, Shiga]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan in Shiga prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Oka-jinja (Shiga)]][[ja:乎加神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Oka-jinja (Shiga)]][[ja:乎加神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Oka-jinja (Shiga)]][[ja:乎加神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11369798]] Wikidata name: Oka-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Oka Shrine | location=Moricho, Jingocho, Higashiomi City, Shiga Prefecture | coordinates={{Coord|35.177055555555555|N|136.18436111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Toyouke no Mikoto
(Associate deity) Hakusan Hime no Mikoto | Rank=Shikinaisha (Minor), Ken-sha | Main Hall=Shinmei-zukuri, Width: 3 ken, Depth: 2 ken | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Oka jinja torii.jpg | prefecture={{ill|Higashiomi|WD=Q240891}} }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Higashiōmi|en|Higashiōmi|WD=Q240891|lt=Higashiomi}}, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, previously Ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke no Mikoto}} as the main deity, along with {{ill|Kukurihime|en|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan Hime no Mikoto}} as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke no Mikoto}}, a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as {{ill|Kukurihime|en|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan Hime no Mikoto}}. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kanzaki District, Hyōgo|en|Kanzaki District, Hyōgo|WD=Q1042045|lt=Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|1=Kawaketamikabe Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553975}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|1=Jingō Kamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590941}}, a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Late Yayoi period}} Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke no Mikoto}} * Associate deity: {{ill|Kukurihime|en|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan Hime no Mikoto}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|en|Notogawa Station|WD=Q1042348|lt=Notogawa Station}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|en|Biwako Line|WD=Q804135|lt=Biwako Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|1=Jingō Kamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590941}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|乎加神社|version=91213086|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:乎加神社]] {{Commons category|Oka-jinja (Shiga)}} {{wikidata link|Q11369798}} [[Category:Buildings in Higashiomi, Shiga]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan in Shiga prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kanzaki District, Hyōgo|en|Kanzaki District, Hyōgo|WD=Q1042045|lt=Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|1=Kawaketamikabe Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553975}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|1=Jingō Kamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590941}}, a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Late Yayoi period}} Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke no Mikoto}} * Associate deity: {{ill|Kukurihime|en|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan Hime no Mikoto}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|en|Notogawa Station|WD=Q1042348|lt=Notogawa Station}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|en|Biwako Line|WD=Q804135|lt=Biwako Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|1=Jingō Kamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590941}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|乎加神社|version=91213086|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:乎加神社]] {{Commons category|Oka-jinja (Shiga)}} {{wikidata link|Q11369798}} [[Category:Buildings in Higashiomi, Shiga]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan in Shiga prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

[[co:commons:Category:Oka-jinja (Shiga)]][[ja:乎加神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Oka-jinja (Shiga)]][[ja:乎加神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Oka-jinja (Shiga)]][[ja:乎加神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Oka Shrine | location=Moricho, Jingocho, Higashiomi City, Shiga Prefecture | coordinates={{Coord|35.177055555555555|N|136.18436111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Toyouke no Mikoto
(Associate deity) Hakusan Hime no Mikoto | Rank=Shikinaisha (Minor), Ken-sha | Main Hall=Shinmei-zukuri, Width: 3 ken, Depth: 2 ken | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Oka jinja torii.jpg | prefecture={{ill|Higashiomi|WD=Q240891}} }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Higashiōmi|en|Higashiōmi|WD=Q240891|lt=Higashiomi}}, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, previously Ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke no Mikoto}} as the main deity, along with {{ill|Kukurihime|en|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan Hime no Mikoto}} as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke no Mikoto}}, a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as {{ill|Kukurihime|en|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan Hime no Mikoto}}. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kanzaki District, Hyōgo|en|Kanzaki District, Hyōgo|WD=Q1042045|lt=Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|1=Kawaketamikabe Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553975}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|1=Jingō Kamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590941}}, a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Late Yayoi period}} Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke no Mikoto}} * Associate deity: {{ill|Kukurihime|en|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan Hime no Mikoto}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, previously Ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke no Mikoto}} as the main deity, along with {{ill|Kukurihime|en|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan Hime no Mikoto}} as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke no Mikoto}}, a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as {{ill|Kukurihime|en|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan Hime no Mikoto}}. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kanzaki District, Hyōgo|en|Kanzaki District, Hyōgo|WD=Q1042045|lt=Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|1=Kawaketamikabe Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553975}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|1=Jingō Kamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590941}}, a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Late Yayoi period}} Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke no Mikoto}} * Associate deity: {{ill|Kukurihime|en|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan Hime no Mikoto}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|en|Notogawa Station|WD=Q1042348|lt=Notogawa Station}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|en|Biwako Line|WD=Q804135|lt=Biwako Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|1=Jingō Kamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590941}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|乎加神社|version=91213086|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:乎加神社]] {{Commons category|Oka-jinja (Shiga)}} {{wikidata link|Q11369798}} [[Category:Buildings in Higashiomi, Shiga]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan in Shiga prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, previously Ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke no Mikoto}} as the main deity, along with {{ill|Kukurihime|en|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan Hime no Mikoto}} as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke no Mikoto}}, a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as {{ill|Kukurihime|en|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan Hime no Mikoto}}. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kanzaki District, Hyōgo|en|Kanzaki District, Hyōgo|WD=Q1042045|lt=Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|1=Kawaketamikabe Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553975}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|1=Jingō Kamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590941}}, a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Late Yayoi period}} Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke no Mikoto}} * Associate deity: {{ill|Kukurihime|en|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan Hime no Mikoto}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|en|Notogawa Station|WD=Q1042348|lt=Notogawa Station}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|en|Biwako Line|WD=Q804135|lt=Biwako Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|1=Jingō Kamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590941}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|乎加神社|version=91213086|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:乎加神社]] {{Commons category|Oka-jinja (Shiga)}} {{wikidata link|Q11369798}} [[Category:Buildings in Higashiomi, Shiga]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan in Shiga prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Oka-jinja (Shiga)]][[ja:乎加神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Oka-jinja (Shiga)]][[ja:乎加神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Oka-jinja (Shiga)]][[ja:乎加神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Oka Shrine | location=Moricho, Jingocho, Higashiomi City, Shiga Prefecture | coordinates={{Coord|35.177055555555555|N|136.18436111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Toyouke no Mikoto
(Associate deity) Hakusan Hime no Mikoto | Rank=Shikinaisha (Minor), Ken-sha | Main Hall=Shinmei-zukuri, Width: 3 ken, Depth: 2 ken | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Oka jinja torii.jpg | prefecture={{ill|Higashiomi|WD=Q240891|1=Higashiōmi}} }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Higashiōmi|en|Higashiōmi|WD=Q240891|lt=Higashiomi|1=Higashiōmi}}, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, previously Ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke no Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} as the main deity, along with {{ill|Kukurihime|en|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}} as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke no Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}}, a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as {{ill|Kukurihime|en|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}}. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kanzaki District, Hyōgo|en|Kanzaki District, Hyōgo|WD=Q1042045|lt=Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province|1=Kanzaki District, Hyōgo}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|1=Kawaketamikabe Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553975}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|1=Jingō Kamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590941}}, a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Late Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke no Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} * Associate deity: {{ill|Kukurihime|en|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|en|Notogawa Station|WD=Q1042348|lt=Notogawa Station|1=Notogawa Station}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|en|Biwako Line|WD=Q804135|lt=Biwako Line|1=Biwako Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|1=Jingō Kamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590941}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|乎加神社|version=91213086|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:乎加神社]] {{Commons category|Oka-jinja (Shiga)}} {{wikidata link|Q11369798}} [[Category:Buildings in Higashiomi, Shiga]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan in Shiga prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

[[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|1=Jingō Kamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590941}}, a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Late Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke no Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} * Associate deity: {{ill|Kukurihime|en|Kukurihime|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|en|Notogawa Station|WD=Q1042348|lt=Notogawa Station|1=Notogawa Station}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|en|Biwako Line|WD=Q804135|lt=Biwako Line|1=Biwako Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|1=Jingō Kamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590941}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center - Oka Shrine] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=816 Shiga Prefecture Shrine Bureau - Oka Shrine] {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|乎加神社|version=91213086|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:乎加神社]] {{Commons category|Oka-jinja (Shiga)}} {{wikidata link|Q11369798}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Higashiomi, Shiga]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan in Shiga prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

[[co:commons:Category:Oka-jinja (Shiga)]][[ja:乎加神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Oka-jinja (Shiga)]][[ja:乎加神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Oka-jinja (Shiga)]][[ja:乎加神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Oka Shrine | location=Moricho, Jingocho, Higashiomi City, Shiga Prefecture | coordinates={{Coord|35.177055555555555|N|136.18436111111112|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Toyouke no Mikoto
(Associate deity) Hakusan Hime no Mikoto | Rank=Shikinaisha (Minor), Ken-sha | Main Hall=Shinmei-zukuri, Width: 3 ken, Depth: 2 ken | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Oka jinja torii.jpg | prefecture={{ill|Higashiomi|WD=Q240891|1=Higashiōmi}} }} '''Oka Shrine''' (乎加神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Higashiōmi|en|Higashiōmi|WD=Q240891|lt=Higashiomi|1=Higashiōmi}}, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, previously Ranked as a Ken-sha (Prefectural Shrine). The shrine enshrines {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke no Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} as the main deity, along with {{ill|Kikurihime|en|Kikurihime|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|1=Kikurihime}} as an associate deity. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke no Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}}, a deity worshipped for bestowing the origins of grains, silkworms, and hemp to humanity, as well as {{ill|Kikurihime|en|Kikurihime|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|1=Kikurihime}}. Listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as one of the two shrines in {{ill|Kanzaki District, Hyōgo|en|Kanzaki District, Hyōgo|WD=Q1042045|lt=Kōzuke District, Ōmi Province|1=Kanzaki District, Hyōgo}} (the other being {{ill|Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|ja|河桁御河邊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaketa Mikawa Shrine|1=Kawaketamikabe Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11553975}}), Oka Shrine is said to have been established on the imperial decree of Emperor Ingyō. According to the shrine's records, the area names such as "Kōzuke District," "Jingo," and the ancient village name "Kannushi" derive from the shrine. [[File:Oka_jinja_information.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Historical Signage of Oka Shrine]] North of the main hall is the {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|1=Jingō Kamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590941}}, a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Late Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} Kofun, which is believed to have been a ritual site in ancient times. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke no Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}} * Associate deity: {{ill|Kikurihime|en|Kikurihime|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan Hime no Mikoto|1=Kikurihime}} == Grounds == [[File:Oka_jinja.jpg|thumb|250px|Oka Shrine Heiden]] In 2003, the main hall[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003249 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], haiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], heiden[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003253 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], norito-den[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003251 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties], temizuya and tamagaki[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/101/00003252 Agency for Cultural Affairs: Database of National Cultural Properties] were registered as {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Japan)|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}}. These structures, built during the Meiji era, include: * '''Main Hall''': Shinmei-zukuri, width 3 ken, depth 2 ken. Originally part of the {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} transferred in 1892 (Meiji 25). * '''Haiden''': Hip-and-gable roof with open walls and floorless construction. * '''Shrine Precincts''': Housing auxiliary shrines like Hiyoshi Shrine, Wakamiya Shrine, Gosha Shrine, Yasaka Shrine, and Wakamiya Shrine. == Festivals == * Closest Sunday to April 9. == Access == * A 20-minute walk from {{ill|Notogawa Station|en|Notogawa Station|WD=Q1042348|lt=Notogawa Station|1=Notogawa Station}} on the {{ill|Biwako Line|en|Biwako Line|WD=Q804135|lt=Biwako Line|1=Biwako Line}}. == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|ja|神郷亀塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Kamezuka Kofun|1=Jingō Kamezuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11590941}} * {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat|Oka-jinja (Shiga)|Oka Shrine}} * [http://www.town.notogawa.shiga.jp/acic/dayori/0302.htm Integrated Cultural Information Center -
